It provides a minimal DOM shim that implements precisely\n15\t * the surface the render helpers touch — document.createElement,\n16\t * document.createTextNode, appendChild, className, classList.add, dataset,\n17\t * textContent (→ escaped innerHTML), and innerHTML get/set — then loads the\n18\t * actual src/canvas/canvas.js and renders the card dicts the canvas really\n19\t * receives from GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics.\n20\t *\n21\t * Two modes:\n22\t *\n23\t *   1. This is the headlessly-testable contract the bead's\n34\t *      acceptance criteria name: \"welcome card renders from a zero-card\n35\t *      session and is dropped on first result.\"\n36\t *\n37\t * The Python contract tests (tests/test_canvas_render.py and\n38\t * tests/e2e/test_canvas_welcome_card.py) feed it JSON and assert the rendered\n39\t * outerHTML, so a regression in the render contract is caught headlessly, in\n40\t * CI, with no browser.\n41\t *\n42\t * Usage:\n43\t *   node canvas_dom_runner.js '<json array of card dicts>'\n44\t *   echo '<json array>' | node canvas_dom_runner.js        # stdin fallback\n45\t *   node canvas_dom_runner.js --container '{\"cards\":[...],\"projects\":[...],\"description\":\"...\"}'\n46\t *   echo '{...}' | node canvas_dom_runner.js --container   # stdin fallback\n47\t *\n48\t * Stdout: a JSON array of { outerHTML, className, dataset } per rendered node.\n49\t */\n50\t\n51\t\"use strict\";\n52\t\n53\tconst fs = require(\"fs\");\n54\tconst path = require(\"path\");\n55\t\n56\t// --- minimal DOM shim -------------------------------------------------------\n57\t\n58\tfunction escapeText(s) {\n59\t    // Matches the browser: textContent→innerHTML escapes &, <, > (not quotes).\n60\t    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/</g, \"&lt;\").replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\");\n61\t}\n62\t\n63\tfunction escapeAttr(s) {\n64\t    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n65\t}\n66\t\n67\t// A text node (document.createTextNode). Its serialized form is the escaped\n68\t// text — the escaping contract's literal form, since el()/createTextNode never\n69\t// splice raw markup.\n70\tclass ShimText {\n71\t    constructor(text) {\n72\t        this._text = text == null ? \"\" : String(text);\n73\t        this.nodeType = 3;\n74\t    }\n75\t\n76\t    set textContent(v) {\n77\t        this._text = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n78\t    }\n79\t\n80\t    get textContent() {\n81\t        return this._text;\n82\t    }\n83\t\n84\t    get outerHTML() {\n85\t        return escapeText(this._text);\n86\t    }\n87\t\n88\t    get innerHTML() {\n89\t        return escapeText(this._text);\n90\t    }\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\tclass ShimNode {\n94\t    constructor(tag) {\n95\t        this._tag = tag;\n96\t        this._classes = [];\n97\t        this._dataset = {};\n98\t        this._textContent = \"\";\n99\t        this._innerHTML = \"\";\n100\t        this._children = []; // built via appendChild (el()/createWelcomeCard path)\n101\t        this.style = {}; // present so style.* assignments are no-ops, not crashes\n102\t    }\n103\t\n104\t    set className(v) {\n105\t        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n106\t    }\n107\t\n108\t    get className() {\n109\t        return this._classes.join(\" \");\n110\t    }\n111\t\n112\t    get classList() {\n113\t        const self = this;\n114\t        return {\n115\t            add(c) {\n116\t                if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c);\n117\t            },\n118\t            contains(c) {\n119\t                return self._classes.includes(c);\n120\t            },\n121\t            toggle(c, force) {\n122\t                const on = force === undefined ? \"\" : String(v);\n154\t        this._children = []; // innerHTML= replaces children, browser-faithful\n155\t    }\n156\t\n157\t    get innerHTML() {\n158\t        // Children built via appendChild serialize recursively; a directly-set\n159\t        // innerHTML string (the createTopicCard path) is used otherwise.\n160\t        if (this._children.length) {\n161\t            return this._children.map((c) => c.outerHTML).join(\"\");\n162\t        }\n163\t        return this._innerHTML;\n164\t    }\n165\t\n166\t    _datasetAttrs() {\n167\t        // dataset.topicId → data-topic-id (camelCase → kebab-case w/ data- prefix).\n168\t        const out = [];\n169\t        for (const key of Object.keys(this._dataset)) {\n170\t            const name = \"data-\" + key.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \"-$1\").toLowerCase();\n171\t            out.push(`${name}=\"${escapeAttr(this._dataset[key])}\"`);\n172\t        }\n173\t        return out;\n174\t    }\n175\t\n176\t    get outerHTML() {\n177\t        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` class=\"${this._classes.join(\" \")}\"` : \"\";\n178\t        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n179\t        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n180\t        return `<${this._tag}${cls}${attrStr}>${this.innerHTML}</${this._tag}>`;\n181\t    }\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\t// canvas.js references `document` as a free variable inside its functions, so\n185\t// it must resolve via the global scope at call time.\n186\tglobal.document = {\n187\t    createElement(tag) {\n188\t        return new ShimNode(tag);\n189\t    },\n190\t    createTextNode(text) {\n191\t        return new ShimText(text);\n192\t    },\n193\t};\n194\t\n195\t// --- load the REAL canvas.js ------------------------------------------------\n196\t\n197\tconst canvasPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"canvas.js\");\n198\tconst canvas = require(canvasPath); // { createTopicCard, escapeHtml, formatStaleness, getStalenessLevel }\n199\t\n200\t// --- render driver ----------------------------------------------------------\n201\t\n202\t// Read the JSON payload for the active mode. argvPos is the index of the\n203\t// positional JSON arg (after any --mode flag); stdin is the pipe-friendly\n204\t// fallback. Returns the parsed value.\n205\tfunction readPayload(argvPos) {\n206\t    if (process.argv[argvPos] !== undefined && process.argv[argvPos] !== \"\") {\n207\t        return JSON.parse(process.argv[argvPos]);\n208\t    }\n209\t    const raw = fs.readFileSync(0, \"utf8\");\n210\t    return JSON.parse(raw);\n211\t}\n212\t\n213\tfunction serialize(node) {\n214\t    return {\n215\t        outerHTML: node.outerHTML,\n216\t        className: node.className,\n217\t        dataset: { ...node.dataset },\n218\t    };\n219\t}\n220\t\n221\t// DEFAULT mode: render an array of card dicts through createTopicCard() — the\n222\t// function loadTopics() calls per card after a reload.\n223\tfunction runCardsMode() {\n224\t    const cards = readPayload(2);\n225\t    if (!Array.isArray(cards)) {\n226\t        throw new Error(\"expected a JSON array of card dicts\");\n227\t    }\n228\t    const rendered = cards.map((card) => serialize(canvas.createTopicCard(card)));\n229\t    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(rendered));\n230\t}\n231\t\n232\t// --container mode (bead adc-4nd25): render buildContainerChildren() — the\n233\t// shared render-path core loadTopics() calls. Empty cards → the welcome card;\n234\t// any card → topic cards only (welcome dropped). The input object mirrors what\n235\t// loadTopics() builds: cards from GET /topics, projects from GET /registry,\n236\t// and an optional one-line description.\n237\tfunction runContainerMode() {\n238\t    const payload = readPayload(3);\n239\t    const cards = (payload && payload.cards) || [];\n240\t    const projects = (payload && payload.projects) || [];\n241\t    const description = payload && payload.description;\n242\t    const nodes = canvas.buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description);\n243\t    const rendered = nodes.map((node) => serialize(node));\n244\t    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(rendered));\n245\t}\n246\t\n247\tfunction main() {\n248\t    if (process.argv[2] === \"--container\") {\n249\t        runContainerMode();\n250\t    } else {\n251\t        runCardsMode();\n252\t    }\n253\t}\n254\t\n255\ttry {\n256\t    main();\n257\t} catch (err) {\n258\t    process.stderr.write(`canvas_dom_runner error: ${err && err.stack ? err.stack : err}\\n`);\n259\t    process.exit(1);\n260\t}\n261\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a10e92cd-3ec1-461e-9611-1adf288a5df5","uuid":"3ab5ab82-148b-443f-a310-a09509e3fd74","timestamp":"2026-07-23T05:15:53.646Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js","content":"#!/usr/bin/env node\n/*\n * Headless DOM runner for the REAL production canvas render module\n * (src/canvas/canvas.js). Bead adc-1l8w; ``--container`` mode (bead adc-4nd25).\n *\n * canvas.js is written so its render helpers (createTopicCard et al.) can be\n * exercised without a browser — its module docstring promises exactly this\n * file:\n *\n *     \"tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js loads this exact file under a minimal\n *      DOM shim and asserts the rendered HTML, with no browser or network\n *      required.\"\n *\n * This is that file. It provides a minimal DOM shim that implements precisely\n * the surface the render helpers touch — document.createElement,\n * document.createTextNode, appendChild, className, classList.add, dataset,\n * textContent (→ escaped innerHTML), and innerHTML get/set — then loads the\n * actual src/canvas/canvas.js and renders the card dicts the canvas really\n * receives from GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics.\n *\n * Two modes:\n *\n *   1. DEFAULT (bead adc-1l8w) — render an array of card dicts through\n *      createTopicCard(), the function loadTopics() calls per card. Drives the\n *      escaping contract for the topic-card family.\n *\n *   2. ``--container`` (bead adc-4nd25) — render the shared container-decision\n *      core buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description), which is what\n *      loadTopics() actually calls: on a fresh session with ZERO cards it\n *      returns the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project\n *      list — no DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it\n *      returns topic cards only (dropping the welcome card, never alongside\n *      real cards). This is the headlessly-testable contract the bead's\n *      acceptance criteria name: \"welcome card renders from a zero-card\n *      session and is dropped on first result.\"\n *\n * The Python contract tests (tests/test_canvas_render.py and\n * tests/e2e/test_canvas_welcome_card.py) feed it JSON and assert the rendered\n * outerHTML, so a regression in the render contract is caught headlessly, in\n * CI, with no browser.\n *\n * Usage:\n *   node canvas_dom_runner.js '<json array of card dicts>'\n *   echo '<json array>' | node canvas_dom_runner.js        # stdin fallback\n *   node canvas_dom_runner.js --container '{\"cards\":[...],\"projects\":[...],\"description\":\"...\"}'\n *   echo '{...}' | node canvas_dom_runner.js --container   # stdin fallback\n *\n * Stdout: a JSON array of { outerHTML, className, dataset } per rendered node.\n */\n\n\"use strict\";\n\nconst fs = require(\"fs\");\nconst path = require(\"path\");\n\n// --- minimal DOM shim -------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeText(s) {\n    // Matches the browser: textContent→innerHTML escapes &, <, > (not quotes).\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/</g, \"&lt;\").replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\");\n}\n\nfunction escapeAttr(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n}\n\n// A text node (document.createTextNode). Its serialized form is the escaped\n// text — the escaping contract's literal form, since el()/createTextNode never\n// splice raw markup.\nclass ShimText {\n    constructor(text) {\n        this._text = text == null ? \"\" : String(text);\n        this.nodeType = 3;\n    }\n\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._text = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n    }\n\n    get textContent() {\n        return this._text;\n    }\n\n    get outerHTML() {\n        return escapeText(this._text);\n    }\n\n    get innerHTML() {\n        return escapeText(this._text);\n    }\n}\n\nclass ShimNode {\n    constructor(tag) {\n        this._tag = tag;\n        this._classes = [];\n        this._dataset = {};\n        this._textContent = \"\";\n        this._innerHTML = \"\";\n        this._children = []; // built via appendChild (el()/createWelcomeCard path)\n        this.style = {}; // present so style.* assignments are no-ops, not crashes\n    }\n\n    set className(v) {\n        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n    }\n\n    get className() {\n        return this._classes.join(\" \");\n    }\n\n    get classList() {\n        const self = this;\n        return {\n            add(c) {\n                if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c);\n            },\n            contains(c) {\n                return self._classes.includes(c);\n            },\n            toggle(c, force) {\n                const on = force === undefined ? !self._classes.includes(c) : !!force;\n                if (on && !self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c);\n                if (!on) self._classes = self._classes.filter((x) => x !== c);\n                return on;\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    get dataset() {\n        return this._dataset;\n    }\n\n    appendChild(node) {\n        this._children.push(node);\n        return node;\n    }\n\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._textContent = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        // Setting textContent replaces all children with a single text node —\n        // mirrors the browser, and keeps escapeHtml()'s \"set textContent then\n        // read innerHTML\" pattern producing escaped text.\n        this._children = [];\n        this._innerHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n    }\n\n    get textContent() {\n        return this._textContent;\n    }\n\n    set innerHTML(v) {\n        this._innerHTML = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        this._children = []; // innerHTML= replaces children, browser-faithful\n    }\n\n    get innerHTML() {\n        // Children built via appendChild serialize recursively; a directly-set\n        // innerHTML string (the createTopicCard path) is used otherwise.\n        if (this._children.length) {\n            return this._children.map((c) => c.outerHTML).join(\"\");\n        }\n        return this._innerHTML;\n    }\n\n    _datasetAttrs() {\n        // dataset.topicId → data-topic-id (camelCase → kebab-case w/ data- prefix).\n        const out = [];\n        for (const key of Object.keys(this._dataset)) {\n            const name = \"data-\" + key.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \"-$1\").toLowerCase();\n            out.push(`${name}=\"${escapeAttr(this._dataset[key])}\"`);\n        }\n        return out;\n    }\n\n    get outerHTML() {\n        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` class=\"${this._classes.join(\" \")}\"` : \"\";\n        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n        return `<${this._tag}${cls}${attrStr}>${this.innerHTML}</${this._tag}>`;\n    }\n}\n\n// canvas.js references `document` as a free variable inside its functions, so\n// it must resolve via the global scope at call time.\nglobal.document = {\n    createElement(tag) {\n        return new ShimNode(tag);\n    },\n    createTextNode(text) {\n        return new ShimText(text);\n    },\n};\n\n// --- load the REAL canvas.js ------------------------------------------------\n\nconst canvasPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"canvas.js\");\nconst canvas = require(canvasPath); // { createTopicCard, escapeHtml, formatStaleness, getStalenessLevel }\n\n// --- render driver ----------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Read the JSON payload for the active mode. argvPos is the index of the\n// positional JSON arg (after any --mode flag); stdin is the pipe-friendly\n// fallback. Returns the parsed value.\nfunction readPayload(argvPos) {\n    if (process.argv[argvPos] !== undefined && process.argv[argvPos] !== \"\") {\n        return JSON.parse(process.argv[argvPos]);\n    }\n    const raw = fs.readFileSync(0, \"utf8\");\n    return JSON.parse(raw);\n}\n\nfunction serialize(node) {\n    return {\n        outerHTML: node.outerHTML,\n        className: node.className,\n        dataset: { ...node.dataset },\n    };\n}\n\n// DEFAULT mode: render an array of card dicts through createTopicCard() — the\n// function loadTopics() calls per card after a reload.\nfunction runCardsMode() {\n    const cards = readPayload(2);\n    if (!Array.isArray(cards)) {\n        throw new Error(\"expected a JSON array of card dicts\");\n    }\n    const rendered = cards.map((card) => serialize(canvas.createTopicCard(card)));\n    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(rendered));\n}\n\n// --container mode (bead adc-4nd25): render buildContainerChildren() — the\n// shared render-path core loadTopics() calls. Empty cards → the welcome card;\n// any card → topic cards only (welcome dropped). That proves the *server* still delivers\n * to a reconnected stream — but it never drives the browser's own EventSource\n * state machine (onopen → loadTopics, onerror, addEventListener('result_created')\n * → loadTopics, the 'disconnect' → eventSource.close()). That second onopen\n * is the canvas's reconnect path, and it calls loadTopics() (the re-render\n * the AC names). We model reconnect by firing onopen again on the live\n * EventSource — faithful to real browser behavior.\n *\n * The Python driver (tests/test_canvas_eventsource_reconnect.py) feeds a JSON\n * test --- minimal DOM shim --------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeText(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/</g, \"&lt;\").replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\");\n}\nfunction escapeAttr(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n}\n\n// --- setup mock document FIRST (before loading canvas.js) --------------------\n\nconst elementsById = {};\n\nclass El {\n    constructor(tag) {\n        this._tag = tag;\n        this._classes = [];\n        this._classHistory = [];\n        this._dataset = this._title = \"\";\n        this._textHistory = [];\n        // For style.display, wrap in a proxy to track changes\n        this._display = \"\";\n    }\n    set className(v) {\n        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n        this._classHistory.push(this._classes.join(\" \"));\n    }\n    get className() { return this._classes.join(\" \"); }\n    get classList() {\n        const self = this;\n        return {\n            add(c) { if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c); },\n            remove(c) { self._classes = self._classes.filter((x) => x !== c); },\n            contains(c) { return self._classes.includes(c); },\n        };\n    }\n    get dataset() { return this._dataset; }\n    get style() {\n        // Return a proxy that tracks style.display changes\n        return new Proxy(this._style, {\n            set: (target, prop, value) => {\n                if (prop === 'display') {\n                    this._display = value;\n                }\n                target[prop] = value;\n                return true;\n            },\n            get: (target, prop) => {\n                if (prop === 'display') {\n                    return this._display;\n                }\n                return target[prop];\n            }\n        });\n    }\n    get disabled() { return this._disabled; }\n this._innerHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n        this._textHistory.push(this._textContent);\n    }\n    get textContent() { return this._textContent; }\n    set innerHTML(v) {\n        this._innerHTML = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        if (this._innerHTML === \"\") this._children = [];   // container.innerHTML = ''\n    }\n    get innerHTML() {\n        if (this._children.length) {\n            return this._children.map((c) => {\n                // Render text nodes (tag === \"#text\") as escaped text\n                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n                    return c._innerHTML || escapeText(c._textContent || \"\");\n                }\n                // Render element nodes via outerHTML\n                return c.outerHTML || \"\";\n            }).join(\"\");\n        }\n        return this._innerHTML;\n    }\n    appendChild(c) {\n        // Handle DocumentFragment: spill its children into this element\n        // Fragments have _children array but no _tag property (elements always have _tag)\n        if (c && c._children && Array.isArray(c._children) && !c._tag) {\n            // Append all children from the fragment\n            c._children.forEach((child) => {\n                // For elements, add them to _children\n                if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n                    this._children.push(child);\n                } else if (child) {\n                    // Text nodes\n                    this._children.push(child);\n                }\n            });\n            return c;\n        }\n        // Regular element or text node - append directly\n        if (c && c._tag !== \"#text\") {\n            this._children.push(c);\n        } else if (c) {\n            // Text nodes are appended but not tracked in _children for querySelector\n            this._children.push(c);  // Keep for innerHTML rendering\n        }\n        return c;\n    }\n    querySelector(selector) {\n        // Minimal querySelector support for class selectors (e.g., \".pending-progress\")\n        if (selector.startsWith(\".\")) {\n            const className = selector.slice(1);\n            // Check this element\n            if (this._classes && this._classes.includes(className)) {\n                return this;\n            }\n            // Recursively search all descendants\n            function searchDeep(children) {\n                for (const child of children) {\n                    // Skip text nodes in search\n                    if (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n\n                    if (child._classes && child._classes.includes(className)) {\n                        return child;\n                    }\n                    if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n                        const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n                        if (found) return found;\n                    }\n                }\n                return null;\n            }\n            // Fall back to searching all elements with matching class\n            function searchAllElements(element) {\n                if (element._classes && element._classes.includes(className)) {\n                    return element;\n                }\n                if (element._children && element._children.length) {\n                    for (const child of element._children) {\n                        if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n                            const found = searchAllElements(child);\n                            if (found) return found;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                return null;\n            }\n            return searchAllElements(this);\n            return searchDeep(this._children);\n        }\n        return null;\n    }\n    addEventListener() {}   // handlers are not driven by this harness\n    _datasetAttrs() {\n        const out = escapeAttr(this._dataset[key]) + '\"');\n        }\n        return out;\n    }\n    get outerHTML() {\n        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` class=\"${this._classes.join(\" \")}\"` : \"\";\n        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n        // For text nodes, just return the escaped text content\n        if (this._tag === \"#text\") {\n            return this._textContent || \"\";\n        }\n        // Render element with its children, innerHTML, or textContent\n        let childrenHTML = \"\";\n        if (this._children && this._children.length) {\n            // Render from children (e.g., after appendChild)\n            childrenHTML = this._children.map((c) => {\n                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n                    return c._textContent ? escapeText(c._textContent) : \"\";\n                }\n                return c.outerHTML || \"\";\n            }).join(\"\");\n        } else (this._textContent) {\n            // Element has direct textContent (set via textContent setter) but no children/innerHTML\n            // This is the case when _setProgress sets node.textContent = \"X/Y sources in\"\n            childrenHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n        }\n        return `<${this._tag}${cls}${attrStr}>${childrenHTML}</${this._tag}>`;\n    }\n    insertBefore(newNode, referenceNode) {\n        if (!referenceNode) {\n            // If referenceNode is null, append to the end\n            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n        }\n        // Find the index of the reference node in _children\n        const refIndex = this._children.indexOf(referenceNode);\n        if (refIndex === -1) {\n            // Reference node not found, append to end\n            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n        }\n        // Insert the new node before the reference node\n        this._children.splice(refIndex, 0, newNode);\n        return newNode;\n    }\n    remove() {\n        // Remove this element from its parent's children\n        // In the mock, we need to find the parent and remove this element\n        // Since elementsById tracks top-level elements, we search for a parent\n        for (const id in elementsById) {\n            const parent = elementsById[id];\n            if (parent._children && parent._children.includes(this)) {\n                parent._children = parent._children.filter((c) => c !== this);\n                return;\n            }\n        }\n        // Also search recursively through all children\n        function removeFromParent(children) {\n            for (let i = 0; i < children.length; i++) {\n                const child = children[i];\n                if (child && child._children && child._children.includes(this)) {\n                    child._children = child._children.filter((c) => c !== this);\n                    return true;\n                }\n                if (child && child._children && child._children.length) {\n                    if (removeFromParent(child._children)) {\n                        return true;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            return false;\n        }\n        for (const id in elementsById) {\n            const parent = elementsById[id];\n            if (parent._children && removeFromParent(parent._children)) {\n                return;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nglobal.document = {\n    querySelector(selector) {\n        // Search through all registered elements for a matching selector\n        // Only supports attribute selectors like [data-pending-id=\"...\"]\n        // Fixed regex to support hyphens in attribute names (was [^-]+ which failed on data-pending-id)\n        if (selector.startsWith('[') && selector.endsWith(']')) {\n            const attrMatch = selector.match(/\\[data-([^\\]]+)=\"([^\"]+)\"\\]/);\n            if (attrMatch) {\n                const attrName = attrMatch[1];\n                const attrValue = attrMatch[2];\n                // Convert attrName from kebab-case to camelCase\n                const camelAttrName = attrName.replace(/-([a-z])/g, (g) => g[1].toUpperCase());\n                // Search through all elements\n                for (const id in elementsById) {\n                    const el = elementsById[id];\n                    if (el._dataset && el._dataset[camelAttrName] === attrValue) {\n                        return el;\n                    }\n                    // Search recursively through children\n                    function searchDeep(children) {\n                        for (const child of children) {\n                            if (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n                            if (child._dataset && child._dataset[camelAttrName] === attrValue) {\n                                return child;\n                            }\n                            if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n                                const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n                                if (found) return found;\n                            }\n                        }\n                        return null;\n                    }\n                    const found = searchDeep(el._children || []);\n                    if (found) return found;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return null;\n    },\n    getElementById(id) {\n        if (!elementsById[id]) elementsById[id] = new El(\"div\");\n        return elementsById[id];\n    },\n    createElement(tag) { return new El(tag); },\n    createTextNode(text) {\n        // Return a minimal text node shim — the el() helper appends these\n        // and our outerHTML render serializes them as escaped text.\n        const node = new El(\"#text\");\n        node._textContent = text == null ? \"\" : String(text);\n        node._innerHTML = escapeText(node._textContent);\n        return node;\n    },\n    createDocumentFragment() {\n        // Return a minimal fragment shim — appendChild tracks children,\n        // and when appended to a real element it spills the children.\n        const frag = {\n            _children: [],\n            childNodes: [],\n            appendChild(child) {\n                this._children.push(child);\n                this.childNodes.push(child);\n                return child;\n            },\n        };\n        return frag;\n    },\n};\n\n// --- mock fetch + EventSource ------------------------------------------------\n\nlet topicsCards = [];\nlet mockSurfaceId = \"surf-mock\";\nconst fetchLog = [];\n\nglobal.fetch = async (url) => {\n    fetchLog.push(url);\n    if (url === \"/openapi.json\") {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ info: { version: \"0.0.0\" } }), text: async () => \"\" };\n    }\n    if (url === \"/api/v1/surfaces/register\") {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ surface_id: mockSurfaceId }) };\n    }\n    if (url.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && url.endsWith(\"/topics\")) {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ cards: topicsCards }) };\n    }\n    if (url === \"/dispatch\") {\n        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n    }\n    return { ok: false, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n};\n\nconst eventSources = [];\nlet closeCount = 0;\n\nclass MockEventSource {\n    constructor(url) {\n        this.url = url;\n        this.readyState = 0;       // 0=connecting 1=open 2=closed\n        this.onopen = null;\n        this.onerror = null;\n        this._listeners = {};\n        this._closed = false;\n        eventSources.push(this);\n    }\n    addEventListener(name, cb) {\n        (this._listeners[name] = this._listeners[name] || []).push(cb);\n    }\n    removeEventListener() {}\n    close() { this._closed = true; this.readyState = 2; closeCount++; }\n    // --- driver helpers (used by the harness, not by the app script) ---\n    fireOpen() { this.readyState = 1; if (this.onopen) this.onopen({}); }\n    fireError(e) { if (this.onerror) this.onerror(e || {}); }\n    dispatch(name, dataObj) {\n        const ev = { data: typeof dataObj === \"string\" ? dataObj : JSON.stringify(dataObj || {}) };\n        (this._listeners[name] || []).forEach((cb) => cb(ev));\n    }\n}\nglobal.EventSource = MockEventSource;\n\n// --- globals the inline script touches ---------------------------------------\n\nglobal.window = {\n    location: { search: \"\", pathname: \"/canvas\" },\n    history: { replaceState() {} },\n    // Deliberately NO SpeechRecognition / webkitSpeechRecognition and NO\n    // navigator.mediaDevices — the inline script's mic block only touches those\n    // inside click handlers, which this harness never fires.\n};\n\n// After canvas.js exports to window, also export those to global scope so\n// bare names like _setProgress work in the inline script (they do in browsers\n// because window is the global scope, but not in Node's vm context).\nfunction syncWindowToGlobal() {\n    if (global.window && typeof global.window === 'object') {\n        for (const key of Object.keys(global.window)) {\n            if (key !== 'location' && key !== 'history') {  // Keep these as objects\n                global[key] = global.window[key];\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n// Node 21+ exposes a read-only `navigator` getter on the global object, so a\n// plain `global.navigator = {}` throws \"which has only a getter\". defineProperty\n// replaces it regardless (Node's getter is configurable). The inline mic block\n// only reads navigator.mediaDevices inside a click handler this harness never\n// fires, but keep a shim so any future top-level navigator read can't crash it.\nObject.defineProperty(global, \"navigator\", { value: {}, configurable: true, writable: true });\n\n// Keep stdout reserved for the single telemetry JSON blob this harness prints at\n// the end. The REAL inline app script uses console.log for debug noise (\"New\n// result:\", \"Server disconnect:\", \"SSE error:\", …); if that reached stdout it\n// would prefix the telemetry and break the Python driver's JSON.parse. Route all\n// console methods to stderr — the same channel the harness's own error path uses.\nglobal.console = Object.assign({}, console, {\n    log: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    error: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    warn: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    info: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n    debug: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n});\nglobal.setInterval = () => 0;   // suppress the 30s heartbeat timer\nglobal.setTimeout = () => 0;\n\n// --- load the REAL render module as globals (as <script src=\"/canvas.js\">) ---\n\nconst canvasPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"canvas.js\");\nconst canvas = require(canvasPath);\nglobal.createTopicCard = canvas.createTopicCard;\nglobal.escapeHtml = canvas.escapeHtml;\nglobal.formatStaleness = canvas.formatStaleness;\nglobal.getStalenessLevel = canvas.getStalenessLevel;\nglobal.buildContainerChildren = canvas.buildContainerChildren;  // Needed by loadTopics()\nglobal._setProgress = canvas._setProgress;  // Internal helper, exported for tests\n// Pending card functions for bead adc-22b1g\nglobal.createPendingPlaceholderCard = canvas.createPendingPlaceholderCard;\nglobal.createPendingThreadCard = canvas.createPendingThreadCard;\nglobal.splitPlaceholderToThreads = canvas.splitPlaceholderToThreads;\nglobal.tickPendingElapsed = canvas.tickPendingElapsed;\nglobal.applyAgedTreatment = canvas.applyAgedTreatment;\nglobal.setPendingProgress = canvas._setProgress;  // Alias for consistency\nglobal.el = canvas.el;  // DOM helper for pending cards\nglobal.createErrorCard = canvas.createErrorCard;  // Error card renderer for SSE error events\n\n// --- extract + run the REAL inline app script --------------------------------\n\nfunction readPlan() {\n    if (process.argv[2] !== undefined && process.argv[2] !== \"\") return JSON.parse(process.argv[2]);\n    return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(0, \"utf8\"));\n}\n\nfunction extractInlineScript(html) {\n    // The main inline app script is the FIRST attribute-less <script> block —\n    // the one immediately after <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> that defines\n    // connectSSE()/loadTopics()/init() and wires the EventSource handlers.\n    // index.html has a LATER attribute-less <script> (the agentation feedback\n    // toolbar, an IIFE that touches document.addEventListener etc.), so taking\n    // the last match would run the wrong script. First match wins.\n    const re = /<script>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/script>/g;\n    const m = re.exec(html);\n    if (!m) throw new Error(\"no attribute-less <script> block found in index.html\");\n    return m[1];\n}\n\nconst microtaskDrain = async (n = 5) => {\n    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) await new Promise((r) => setImmediate(r));\n};\n\nasync function run() {\n    const plan = readPlan();\n    topicsCards = Array.isArray(plan.cards) ? plan.cards : [];\n    mockSurfaceId = plan.register_surface_id || \"surf-mock\";\n    if (plan.session_id) global.window.location.search = `?session_id=${plan.session_id}`;\n    if (plan.openapi_version) {\n        const v = plan.openapi_version;\n        global.fetch = async (url) => {\n            fetchLog.push(url);\n            if (url === \"/openapi.json\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ info: { version: v } }), text: async () => \"\" };\n            if (url === \"/api/v1/surfaces/register\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ surface_id: mockSurfaceId }) };\n            if (url.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && url.endsWith(\"/topics\")) return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ cards: topicsCards }) };\n            if (url === \"/dispatch\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n            return { ok: false, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n        };\n    }\n\n    const htmlPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"index.html\");\n    const inline = extractInlineScript(fs.readFileSync(htmlPath, \"utf8\"));\n    // runInThisContext so the inline script's free `document`/`window`/`fetch`/\n    // `EventSource` resolve to the globals installed above — exactly the globals\n    // a browser would provide. The script's top-level init() call schedules the\n    // async registerSurface() → connectSSE() chain.\n    vm.runInThisContext(inline, { filename: \"canvas-inline-script.js\" });\n\n    // Let init() settle: openapi fetch → registerSurface fetch → connectSSE()\n    // creates the (mock) EventSource and wires its handlers.\n    await microtaskDrain(10);\n\n    for (const step of plan.steps || []) {\n        const es = eventSources[eventSources.length - 1];\n        switch (step.action) {\n            case \"wait\":\n                break;\n            case \"open\":\n                if (es) es.fireOpen();\n                break;\n            case \"error\":\n                if (es) es.fireError();\n                break;\n            case \"event\":\n                if (es) es.dispatch(step.name, step.data);\n                break;\n            case \"close\":\n                if (es) es.close();\n                break;\n            case \"reconnect\":\n                // Native EventSource auto-reconnect: same object fires onopen again.\n                if (es) es.fireOpen();\n                break;\n            case \"setCards\":\n                topicsCards = Array.isArray(step.cards) ? step.cards : [];\n                break;\n            default:\n                throw new Error(`unknown step action: ${step.action}`);\n        }\n        await microtaskDrain(5);\n    }\n\n    // --- telemetry ---\n    const statusText = elementsById[\"statusText\"];\n    const statusDot = elementsById[\"statusDot\"];\n    const textHist = statusText ? statusText._textHistory : [];\n    const dotHist = statusDot ? statusDot._classHistory : [];\n    const statuses = textHist.map((text, i) => {\n        const dot = dotHist[i] || \"\";\n        return { status: dot.replace(/^status-dot\\s*/, \"\").trim(), text };\n    });\n\n    const container = elementsById[\"topicsContainer\"];\n    const containerHTML = container ? container.innerHTML : \"\";\n    // card labels live in the rendered <topic-header> text; pull them out of the\n    // HTML the real createTopicCard() produced.\n    const containerCardLabels = (containerHTML.match(/<div class=\"topic-header\">([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/div>/g) || [])\n        .map((h) => {\n            // topic-label is a <div> in canvas.js, not a <span>; match the class\n            // and grab visible text up to the next tag so this is tag-agnostic.\n            const m = h.match(/class=\"topic-label\">\\s*([^<]+)/);\n            return m ? m[1].trim() : \"\";\n        });\n\n    // Fallback: if the label class name shifts, best-effort labels from dataset.\n    const containerCardCount = (containerHTML.match(/class=\"topic-card/g) || []).length;\n\n    // Count pending cards in the container (placeholders + threads)\n    const pendingPlaceholderCount = (containerHTML.match(/data-pending-kind=\"placeholder\"/g) || []).length;\n    const pendingThreadCount = (containerHTML.match(/data-pending-kind=\"thread\"/g) || []).length;\n    const pendingCardCount = (containerHTML.match(/class=\"[^\"]*pending-card[^\"]*\"/g) || []).length;\n\n    // Extract pending card details for testing\n    const pendingCards = [];\n    const pendingCardMatches = containerHTML.match(/<div class=\"[^\"]*builtin-card pending-card[^\"]*\"[^>]*>[\\s\\S]*?<\\/div>/g) || [];\n    pendingCardMatches.forEach((cardHTML) => {\n        const pendingIdMatch = cardHTML.match(/data-pending-id=\"([^\"]+)\"/);\n        const pendingKindMatch = cardHTML.match(/data-pending-kind=\"([^\"]+)\"/);\n        const utteranceMatch = cardHTML.match(/<p class=\"pending-utterance\">([^<]*)<\\/p>/);\n        pendingCards.push({\n            pendingId: pendingIdMatch ? pendingIdMatch[1] : null,\n            pendingKind: pendingKindMatch ? pendingKindMatch[1] : null,\n            utterance: utteranceMatch ? utteranceMatch[1].trim() : null,\n        });\n    });\n\n    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({\n        initCompleted: eventSources.length > 0,\n        eventSourcesCreated: eventSources.length,\n        currentEventSourceUrl: eventSources.length ? eventSources[eventSources.length - 1].url : null,\n        loadTopicsCalls: fetchLog.filter((u) => u.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && u.endsWith(\"/topics\")).length,\n        statuses,\n        closeCalls: closeCount,\n        containerHTML,\n        containerCardCount,\n        containerCardLabels,\n        // Pending card telemetry for bead adc-22b1g\n        pendingPlaceholderCount,\n        pendingThreadCount,\n        pendingCardCount,\n        pendingCards,\n    }));\n}\n\nrun().catch((err) => {\n    process.stderr.write(`canvas_eventsource_runner error: ${err && err.stack ? err.stack : err}\\n`);\n    process.exit(1);\n});\n","numLines":648,"startLine":1,"totalLines":648}}}

No dynamic value is ever\n * spliced raw into innerHTML. It splits into per-thread pending\n * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n *\n * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n    card.classList.add('thread');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n *\n * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n */\nfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n    });\n}\n\n// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\nfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n    }\n\n    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n    progress.style.display = 'none';\n    card.appendChild(progress);\n\n    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n    ]);\n    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n\n    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\nfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n    if (!node || !progress) return;\n    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n}\n\n/**\n * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n */\nfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n    return elapsedMs;\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n */\nfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n    return aged;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n//     from SSE error events\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\nvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n    router_unavailable: {\n        title: 'Router unavailable',\n        icon: '🔌',\n        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n    },\n    all_sources_failed: {\n        title: 'No data available',\n        icon: '📭',\n        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n    },\n    synthesis_failed: {\n        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n        icon: '⚠️',\n        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n    },\n    malformed_router_output: {\n        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n        icon: '🧩',\n        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n    },\n    no_match: {\n        title: 'No matching project',\n        icon: '❓',\n        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n    }\n};\n\nfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n}\n\n/**\n * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n *\n * @param {object} data  {error_type, utterance, detail, sources, data, intent_id, pending_id}\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n *\n * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n */\nfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];","numLines":200,"startLine":300,"totalLines":672}}}

render-contract test for the first-run welcome card (bead adc-4nd25).\n\nThe welcome card builder (``createWelcomeCard``) ships in the served frontend\nmodule ``src/canvas/canvas.js``; this bead WIRING job makes ``loadTopics()``\nactually render it on a fresh session (zero topics) instead of the bare\n\"No active topics\" empty-state. The headlessly-testable core of that wiring is\n``buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description)``: empty ``cards`` → the\nwelcome card; any real card → topic cards only (welcome dropped, never shown\nalongside a real card).\n\nThis suite drives that exact function through the Node DOM runner\n(``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js --container``) under a minimal DOM shim — no\nbrowser, no live server, no DB — and asserts the bead's acceptance criteria:\n\n* a zero-card (fresh) session renders the welcome card, its registered-project\n  list, and **>=2** example utterances;\n* the welcome card is **replaced, not duplicated**, the moment the first real\n  result lands (a non-empty card set yields topic cards only);\n* every interpolated value is a text node — markup in a registry slug or\n  description surfaces as visible text, never as injected HTML (the escaping\n  contract, bead adc-3ixa).\n\nIt is the Python companion the runner's own docstring names\n(\"tests/e2e/test_canvas_welcome_card.py\"), mirroring how\n``tests/test_canvas_dom_verify.py`` covers the topic-card family.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom html.parser import HTMLParser\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import node_available, render_container\n\npytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive canvas DOM runner\"\n)\n\n\n# --- minimal selector-style DOM query over stdlib html.parser ------------------\n# BeautifulSoup/lxml are intentionally not a dependency; createWelcomeCard()\n# emits simple, well-formed HTML we parse with the standard library. This\n# mirrors the query style in tests/test_canvas_dom_verify.py.\n\n\nclass _Node:\n    __slots__ = (\"tag\", \"attrs\", \"classes\", \"children\", \"text_parts\")\n\n    def __init__(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, str | None]]) -> None:\n        self.tag = tag\n        self.attrs = {k: (v if v is not None else \"\") for k, v in attrs}\n        cls = self.attrs.get(\"class\", \"\")\n        self.classes = set(cls.split()) if cls else set()\n        self.children: list[_Node] = []\n        self.text_parts: list[str] = []\n\n    def _walk(self):\n        for child in self.children:\n            yield child\n            yield from child._walk()\n\n    def find_all(self, *, tag: str | None = None, class_: str | None = None) -> list[\"_Node\"]:\n        out: list[_Node] = []\n        for node in self._walk():\n            if tag is not None and node.tag != tag:\n                continue\n            if class_ is not None and class_ not in node.classes:\n                continue\n            out.append(node)\n        return out\n\n    def find(self, *, tag: str | None = None, class_: str | None = None) -> \"_Node | None\":\n        matches = self.find_all(tag=tag, class_=class_)\n        return matches[0] if matches else None\n\n    def has_class(self, class_: str) -> bool:\n        return class_ in self.classes\n\n    @property\n    def text(self) -> str:\n        parts = list(self.text_parts)\n        for node in self._walk():\n            parts.extend(node.text_parts)\n        return \" \".join(p for p in parts if p).strip()\n\n\n_VOID = {\"area\", \"base\", \"br\", \"col\", \"embed\", \"hr\", \"img\", \"input\",\n         \"link\", \"meta\", \"param\", \"source\", \"track\", \"wbr\"}\n\n\nclass _TreeBuilder(HTMLParser):\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__(convert_charrefs=True)\n        self.root = _Node(\"__root__\", [])\n        self._stack: list[_Node] = [self.root]\n\n    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):\n        node = _Node(tag, attrs)\n        self._stack[-1].children.append(node)","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":260}}}

Cross-family escaping audit\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef test_cross_family_escaping_audit():\n    \"\"\"\n    Escaping AUDIT: Every client-filled value across all four families is text node/escapeHtml'd.\n\n    This binds the escaping-contract bead adc-3ixa.\n\n    For each family, we verify that potentially malicious input (HTML, scripts)\n    is escaped and appears as visible text, not executed markup.\n\n    Test cases:\n    - Welcome card: malicious project slug/description\n    - Pending card: malicious utterance\n    - Error card: malicious utterance, detail, source names\n    - Topic card: malicious label, summary, data values\n    \"\"\"\n    malicious_inputs = [\n        \"<script>alert('xss')</script>\",\n        \"<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>\",\n        \"&lt;script&gt;\",\n        \"'; DROP TABLE cards; --\",\n    ]\n\n    # Test welcome card escaping\n    projects = [{\"slug\": \"<script>xss</script>\", \"name\": \"Safe Name\",\n                 \"description\": \"<img onerror=alert(1)>\", \"intent_support\": [\"status\"]}]\n    rendered = render_container(cards=[], projects=projects)\n    html = rendered[0][\"outerHTML\"]\n\n    # Verify malicious content is escaped\n    assert \"<script>\" not in html or \"&lt;script&gt;\" in html\n    assert \"<img\" not in html or \"&lt;img\" in html\n    assert \"onerror\" not in html or \"alert\" not in html\n\n    # Test pending card escaping\n    script = f\"\"\"\n    const canvas = require('/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/canvas/canvas.js');\n    const card = canvas.createPendingPlaceholderCard(\n        '<script>alert(\"xss\")</script>',\n        1000000,\n        'pending-123'\n    );\n    console.log(card.outerHTML);\n    \"\"\"\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        [\"node\", \"-e\", script],\n        capture_output=True,\n        cwd=\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\",\n        timeout=10,\n    )\n\n    if result.returncode == 0:\n        html = result.stdout.decode()\n        # Script tag should be escaped\n        assert \"<script>\" not in html or \"&lt;script&gt;\" in html\n\n    # Test error card escaping\n    plan = {\n        \"session_id\": \"test\",\n        \"register_surface_id\": \"surf-test\",\n        \"openapi_version\": \"0.0.0\",\n        \"cards\": [],\n        \"steps\": [\n            {\"action\": \"open\"},\n            {\"action\": \"event\", \"name\": \"error\", \"data\": {\n                \"error_type\": \"router_unavailable\",\n                \"utterance\": \"<script>xss</script>\",\n                \"detail\": \"<img onerror=alert(1)>\",\n            }},\n        ],\n    }\n\n    plan_json = json.dumps(plan)\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        [\"node\", tests skip cleanly when absent.\nNODE = shutil.which(\"node\")\n\n\ndef node_available() -> bool:\n    \"\"\"True iff a ``node`` binary is on PATH (the DOM runner needs it).\"\"\"\n    return NODE is not None\n\n\ndef render_cards(cards: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"Render card dicts through the REAL canvas.js via the headless DOM runner.\n\n    Feeds ``cards`` (the exact shape ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``\n    returns under ``.cards`` — i.e. what ``loadTopics()`` hands to\n    ``createTopicCard()``) to ``canvas_dom_runner.js`` on stdin and returns its\n    JSON output: one ``{outerHTML, className, dataset}`` per card.\n\n    Raises ``RuntimeError`` if node is missing or the runner exits non-zero.\n    \"\"\"\n    if NODE is None:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"node not found on PATH — cannot drive canvas DOM runner\")\n    proc = subprocess.run(\n        [NODE, str(DOM_RUNNER)],\n        input=json.dumps(cards),\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        timeout=15,\n        check=False,\n    )\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"canvas_dom_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n        )\n    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n\n\ndef render_card(card: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Render a single card dict; returns its ``{outerHTML, className, dataset}``.\"\"\"\n    return render_cards([card])[0]\n\n\ndef render_container(\n    cards: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,\n    projects: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,\n    description: str | None = None,\n) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"Render the shared container-decision core ``loadTopics()`` calls (bead adc-4nd25).\n\n    Drives ``canvas_dom_runner.js --container``, which runs\n    ``buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description)`` — the headlessly-\n    testable core of ``loadTopics()``: a fresh session with ZERO cards yields\n    the first-run welcome card (built from the registry ``projects`` list, never\n    the DB); the moment any real card exists it yields topic cards only,\n    dropping the welcome card. The payload mirrors exactly what\n    ``loadTopics()`` assembles after ``GET /topics`` + ``GET /registry``.\n\n    Returns one ``{outerHTML, className, dataset}`` per rendered container child.\n    Raises ``RuntimeError`` if node is missing or the runner exits non-zero.\n    \"\"\"\n    if NODE is None:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"node not found on PATH — cannot drive canvas DOM runner\")\n    payload = {\n        \"cards\": cards if cards is not None else [],\n        \"projects\": projects if projects is not None else [],\n    }\n    if description is not None:\n        payload[\"description\"] = description\n    proc = subprocess.run(\n        [NODE, str(DOM_RUNNER), \"--container\"],\n        input=json.dumps(payload),\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        timeout=15,\n        check=False,\n    )\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"canvas_dom_runner --container exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n        )\n    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n\n\ndef parse_sse_stream(text: str) -> list[tuple[str, dict]]:\n    \"\"\"Parse raw SSE wire text into ``(event_type, data)`` pairs.\n\n    Mirrors the browser ``EventSource`` contract: blocks separated by a blank\n    line, each block an ``event:`` line and a ``data:`` JSON line. The canvas\n    wires ``result_created`` and ``topic_updated`` blocks to ``loadTopics()``.\n    \"\"\"\n    events: list[tuple[str, dict]] = []\n    for block in text.split(\"\\n\\n\"):\n        block = block.strip()\n        if not block:\n            continue\n        etype = \"\"\n        data: dict = {}\n        for line in block.splitlines():\n            if line.startswith(\"event:\"):\n                etype = line[len(\"event:\"):].strip()\n            elif line.startswith(\"data:\"):\n                payload = line[len(\"data:\"):].strip()\n                try:\n                    data = json.loads(payload)\n                except json.JSONDecodeError:\n                    # Non-JSON data line — preserve raw so callers can still see it.\n                    data = {\"_raw\": payload}\n        if etype:\n            events.append((etype, data))\n    return events\n","numLines":141,"startLine":1,"totalLines":141}}}

subprocess.run(\n        [NODE, str(ES_RUNNER)],\n        input=json.dumps(plan),\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        timeout=15,\n        check=False,\n    )\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n        )\n    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n\n\ndef 5,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A card dict in the shape GET /topics returns under .cards.\"\"\"\n    level = \"fresh\" if seconds < 600 else (\"stale\" if seconds < 3600 else \"very-stale\")\n    return scripts)\n    is escaped and appears as visible text, not executed markup.\n\n    Test cases:\n    - Welcome card: malicious project slug/description\n    - Pending card: malicious utterance\n    -
